calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-26-2023 Vacated

Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendants To Provide Further Verified Responses, Without Objections, To Plaintiffs Request For Admissions (Set One), And For A Monetary Sanction
Off Calendar Jan-25-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):

Atty. Andrew E. Wesley, Esq., LAW OFFICE OF ANDREW WESTLEY (Tele: 415-362-2817) for Plaintiff;
Atty. Genette Genetti, Esq., SELLAR HAZARD & LUCIA (Tel: 925-938-1430) for the Defendants.

Prior to the hearing Pro Tem Judg Catalanotti gave DISCLOSURE. Parties have no objections.

PLAINTIFF JOEL BRUNK, LAURA BRUNK'S Motion To Compel Defendants To Provide Further Verified Responses, Without Objections, To Plaintiffs Request For Admissions (Set One), And For A Monetary Sanction

Matter is argued. A ruling submitted.

The motion is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part; the sanctions portion of the motion is taken under submission.

The prevailing party, Andrew Westley, Esq., is required to prepare a proposed order, in Word format, repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling, approved as to form, and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem Catalanotti at Peter.Catalanotti@wilsonelser.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: R. Sahatjian / M. Goodman; Not Reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendants To Provide Further Verified Responses, Without Objections, To Plaintiffs Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents (Set One), And For A Monetary Sanction
Off Calendar Jan-25-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):

Atty. Andrew E. Wesley, Esq., LAW OFFICE OF ANDREW WESTLEY (Tele: 415-362-2817) for Plaintiff;
Atty. Genette Genetti, Esq., SELLAR HAZARD & LUCIA (Tel: 925-938-1430) for the Defendants.

Prior to the hearing Pro Tem Judg Catalanotti gave DISCLOSURE. Parties have no objections.

PLAINTIFF JOEL BRUNK, LAURA BRUNK'S Motion To Compel Defendants To Provide Further Verified Responses, Without Objections, To Plaintiffs Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents (Set One), And For A Monetary Sanction

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter is argued. A ruling submitted.

The motion is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part; the sanctions portion of the motion is taken under submission.

The prevailing party, Andrew Westley, Esq., is required to prepare a proposed order, in Word format, repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling, approved as to form and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem Catalanotti at Peter.Catalanotti@wilsonelser.com.
